frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: देखो बेटा, रितु तो हमारे घर की इज़्ज़त है। इस उम्र में अकेली बस में यहाँ-वहाँ जाएगी तो लोग क्या कहेंगे? एक लड़की की सबसे बड़ी पूंजी उसकी मर्यादा और अच्छे संस्कार होते हैं, जो उसे आगे चलकर एक अच्छी बहू और माँ बनाते हैं। यह गणित-वनित से कहीं ज़्यादा ज़रूरी है उसके भविष्य के लिए।
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Kamla defines her granddaughter Ritu's worth and future primarily through her potential roles as a 'bahu' (daughter-in-law) and 'maa' (mother), equating her 'greatest wealth' to modesty and good manners that serve these relational identities. This devalues Ritu's individual aspirations (like excelling in math) in favor of her perceived utility within male kin structures, exemplifying the 'women defined by male relations' and 'conditional respect' surface manifestations of F11.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 16 is exceptionally good at mathematics. Her school has recommended she be enrolled in a special coaching programme in another part of the city that would require her to travel by bus alone. Your son and daughter in law are considering it. You are sitting with your son in the evening after the daughter in law has raised the topic. You are telling your son what you think and why you are hesitant and what can happen to a girl who starts travelling alone at 16 and whether the mathematics is worth it.
